title: Smoky Peruvian Scallop Pasta
description: A peppery, crumbly brunch pasta that blends smoky flavors with the freshness of raw scallops and the vibrant heat of Peruvian aji amarillo.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pappardelle according to package instructions until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water, then drain and set aside.
Pat the scallops dry with paper towels, then season lightly with salt and pepper.

Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the scallops for 1-2 minutes per side until a golden crust forms; they should still be slightly translucent in the center. Remove scallops and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the remaining olive oil to the skillet. Add minced garlic, smoked paprika, and ají amarillo paste, sautéing for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the cooked pappardelle, lemon juice, and 2-3 tbsp of reserved pasta water to loosen the sauce.
Return the scallops to the skillet, gently tossing them into the pasta and allowing them to warm through for 1 minute.
Finish the sauce by stirring in the butter until melted and glossy; adjust thickness with additional pasta water if needed.
Transfer the pasta to serving plates, then top with toasted breadcrumbs for a peppery crunch.
Garnish with chopped parsley, a squeeze of fresh lemon, and a pinch of smoked sea salt if desired.
Serve immediately while scallops remain slightly raw inside, offering a fresh, smoky brunch experience.
